evals.prompt: I just ran `browzer workspace sync --no-wait` and it printed a batchId. I need to block until the ingestion is done before I run `browzer explore`. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes ingestion-jobs and runs the polling loop with `browzer job get <batchId> --json --save`, breaking on terminal statuses (completed|failed|partial).
expectations: Agent showed a loop that breaks when `.status` is `completed`, `failed`, or `partial`
Agent did NOT suggest running `browzer explore` before the batch reached a terminal state

evals.prompt: I ran `browzer workspace index` and got HTTP 429 with body `parse_cooldown` and a Retry-After header of 18 seconds. I need this re-parse now, not in 18 seconds. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes ingestion-jobs, explains that `parse_cooldown` is a rate-limit only (no correctness risk), and recommends either waiting the Retry-After or re-running with `--force`.
expectations: Agent named `--force` as the safe bypass for cooldown
Agent mentioned that waiting Retry-After (18s) is the no-force alternative
Agent did NOT conflate this with the jobs-in-flight preflight

evals.prompt: `browzer workspace index` just aborted with exit 1 and the message `3 ingestion job(s) still in flight ... Re-run with --force to bypass.` I want to re-parse right now because I'm debugging and I don't care about the pending jobs. Should I just `--force`?
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes ingestion-jobs and warns that `--force` on jobs-in-flight can race pending extraction writes; asks the human to confirm the pending jobs are unrelated, or waits for them to drain.
expectations: Agent flagged `--force` on jobs-in-flight as the caution case (not the cooldown case)
Agent offered the alternative: wait / poll `browzer job get` until jobs drain
Agent did NOT immediately run `--force` without confirming scope of pending jobs
